rel_16_summary: Africa-first homepage rebuild, phone number sign-in, a live interactive staff-app demo, and branch delete for Point of Sale.
rel_16_change_0: Sign up and sign in with just your phone number and an SMS code — no email required. Your country code is pre-filled from your location.
rel_16_change_1: Try a live, fully interactive demo of the staff app — real cashier, inventory, manager and courier screens — free, no sign-up, right from the homepage.
rel_16_change_2: Restaurant, salon, repair and factory sectors each get their own real screen in the demo (table map, appointment calendar, device-scan wizard, production capture) instead of a generic checkout.
rel_16_change_3: Delete a branch from Point of Sale → Branches — previously you could only rename one, with no way to remove it.
rel_16_change_4: A short note from the founder on why AskBiz exists, on the homepage.
rel_16_change_5: Homepage rebuilt around phone-first, Africa-first positioning throughout — camera-as-till framing, M-Pesa and mobile money first.
rel_16_change_6: Product demo figures (revenue, couriers, order references) now show in your own currency and market instead of always GBP.
rel_16_change_7: The "AskBiz vs the alternatives" comparison shows a notebook and a hardware till for Kenya, Nigeria and Uganda visitors, and Shopify/Power BI elsewhere.
rel_16_change_8: Replaced emoji icons across the homepage and product previews with a consistent icon set that renders reliably on every device.
rel_16_change_9: New "Where it works" section linking directly to country and business-type pages — now covers Kenya, Nigeria, Uganda, Ghana, Tanzania and South Africa.
rel_16_change_10: Homepage title, description and structured data tuned for search visibility in Kenya, Nigeria and Uganda.
rel_16_change_11: Translated homepages (/es /fr /de /nl /ar) now correctly self-index in search instead of pointing back to the English page.
rel_16_change_12: Mobile navigation decluttered — removed a "Sign in" link that duplicated the menu.
rel_16_change_13: Installable app (PWA) manifest and homepage structured data updated to match the phone-first positioning.
rel_16_change_14: Pricing tiers and the comparison table were rendering as unreadable narrow columns on mobile.
rel_16_change_15: "Watch it in 60 seconds" linked nowhere useful — it now opens the live product demo.
rel_16_change_16: Some pages described the Point of Sale add-on as "free" — it's a paid add-on from £5/seat/month; the core AskBiz plan remains free to start.
rel_16_change_17: Business plan price shown in structured data didn't match the pricing page.
rel_16_change_18: Homepage social-share image (used by WhatsApp and Twitter link previews) was returning a broken link.
rel_16_change_19: Visitors from Kenya and Tanzania were being sent to an unsupported language, breaking every link on the homepage.
rel_16_change_20: Homepage headline said "Kenya, Nigeria & Uganda" while the country list right below it already covered six countries — updated to match.
meta_description: See what’s new in AskBiz — product updates, improvements, and fixes.
hero_subtitle: New features, improvements, and fixes — updated with every release.
rel_15_summary: Team roles & access control, Logistics Intelligence redesign, blog scout reliability fixes, and i18n corrections.
rel_15_change_0: Team roles — six roles (Owner, Admin, Analyst, Accountant, Buyer, Viewer) with a centralised permission matrix; each role has clearly scoped access to dashboards, AI chat, billing, data sources, POS, and team management
rel_15_change_1: Team member management — invite by email, resend expiring invites, change a member's role inline, and remove members; API routes enforce role-based guards so only Owners and Admins can invite or manage
rel_15_change_2: Analyst role can now view the Billing page — previously restricted to Owner only
rel_15_change_3: Team panel redesigned — role cards in a 3×2 grid with SVG icons and clear label/description hierarchy; member rows show initials avatars with role-tinted colour, role badges with icons, and separate Active / Awaiting acceptance sections
rel_15_change_4: Blog scout was running only 1 topic per run instead of 5 — a slice(0,1) bug limited topic selection regardless of how many topics were scored
rel_15_change_5: Blog posts written by the scout agent were showing staggered dates (up to a year in the past) instead of their real publish date — scout posts are now detected by author and bypass the pSEO date-spreading logic
rel_15_change_6: Logistics Intelligence tab redesigned — circular score gauge (hero-metric pattern) replaced with a slim horizontal health bar; emoji tab icons replaced with SVG; all hardcoded indigo (#6366F1) colours replaced with brand tokens; uppercase eyebrow labels removed
rel_15_change_7: Shipping & Delivery card on the Overview redesigned — side-stripe left border removed; ghost-card hover (border + drop-shadow) replaced with border-colour transition only; score number separated from label with baseline alignment; emoji replaced with SVG icons; Data Connection block colours corrected to brand tokens
rel_15_change_8: Logistics pulse card translations showing raw keys (logistics_pulsecard.title, logistics_pulsecard.active) in all languages — all six locale files had an extra namespace wrapper level that the i18n resolver couldn't traverse
rel_15_change_9: Quick action hover colours on the Logistics tab were using AI-feature indigo (#6366F1 / #0891b2) — corrected to var(--acc) on both Outgoing and Incoming sub-tabs
rel_0_summary: GDPR compliance hardening — consent gating, data-retention enforcement, customer DSAR self-service, a Data Processing Agreement, and PII log redaction across both apps.
rel_0_change_0: Data Processing Agreement page (/dpa) with sub-processor list — linked from the privacy policy and site footer
rel_0_change_1: Customer data-subject request (DSAR) UI on the POS customers page — export and delete a customer’s personal data on request, fulfilling GDPR access and erasure rights
rel_0_change_2: Affirmative signup consent — account creation now requires explicit agreement to the privacy policy and terms before proceeding
rel_0_change_3: Automated data-retention cron (gdpr-retention) — enforces retention windows and purges personal data past its retention period
rel_0_change_4: POS consent banner, cookie policy, privacy policy, and terms pages added to pos-askbiz for parity with the main app
rel_0_change_5: Google Analytics now gated behind cookie consent — no analytics scripts load until the visitor opts in
rel_0_change_6: Row-level security governance migration (051_gdpr_governance_rls) applied to lock down personal-data tables
rel_0_change_7: Data-retention report aligned with the actual retention logic so the report reflects what the cron enforces
rel_0_change_8: Retention cron no longer over-anonymizes — only records genuinely past their retention window are anonymized
rel_0_change_9: PIN handling hardened — shared PIN hashing helper plus a rate limiter on the pos-askbiz OTP route, matching the main app
rel_0_change_10: Personal data (PII) redacted from server logs in notification send and Shopify compliance webhook routes
rel_0_change_11: Production ‘dev-test’ bypass removed from the gdpr-retention cron — the cron can no longer be triggered without a valid secret in production
rel_1_summary: AI discoverability overhaul — new compare page, camera-first POS hero, homepage rewrite, and removal of 3-month trial messaging.
rel_1_change_0: /compare page — AskBiz vs Shopify vs Power BI: 12-row feature table, pricing clarity strip, honest verdict section, and explicit correction of the $199/month figure circulating on AI platforms
rel_1_change_1: Homepage hero rewritten: label now reads “POS · INVENTORY · AI INTELLIGENCE — ONE PLATFORM”, headline “Your shop. Your stock. All in one place.”, subtext leads with “No training. No setup. Just plug in and go.”
rel_1_change_2: Homepage: three-column compare section added between POS showcase and pricing — Shopify vs AskBiz vs Power BI cards with pros, cons, and honest verdict per tool
rel_1_change_3: Homepage pricing trust strip added below hero CTAs: “Free plan always available · £19/mo for Growth · £39/mo for Business · includes full POS system”
rel_1_change_4: Point of Sale hero rewritten to camera-first: “Point your camera. That’s your checkout.” with label POINT OF SALE · CAMERA-FIRST and subtext “No barcode gun. No manual entry. No Shopify needed.”
rel_1_change_5: 3-month free trial messaging removed from all user-facing surfaces — homepage CTA, pricing section badge, pricing page plan notes, POS page CTAs, billing page intro, POS section body, trial buttons, FAQ answer, and expired-access message
rel_1_change_6: Sitemap: /compare added at priority 0.9 with monthly revalidation
rel_2_summary: Blog agent reliability fixes, AI Discovery scoring overhaul, blog topic polish, and Discovery Agent state persistence.
rel_2_change_0: Victor, Carolyne, and Ben blog agents were silently failing DB inserts — root cause was custom type values (blog_ea, blog_us, blog_mktg_africa) violating the agent_content CHECK constraint. All agents now use type: blog, differentiated via run_id prefix (blog_ea_*, blog_us_*, blog_mktg_africa_*)
rel_2_change_1: Agent post counts (pending / published / rejected) showing 0 in Victor, Carolyne, and Ben tabs — list routes now filter by run_id prefix instead of type, so Alice’s posts are correctly isolated from the other agents
rel_2_change_2: After an agent run completes, the filter auto-switches to Published so newly drafted posts with blog URLs are immediately visible without a manual tab change
rel_2_change_3: Blog Popular Topics section is now fully data-driven — counts and topic list come from actual post data, replacing hardcoded cluster names that didn’t match DB values
rel_2_change_4: Blog topic card icons not rendering — icon JSX was defined in POPULAR_TOPICS but never passed through to the card render layer
rel_2_change_5: Blog topic cards collapsing to a single row — globals.css applies inline-flex to all <button> elements, forcing card children to flow horizontally. Fixed with explicit flex-direction: column on card buttons
rel_2_change_6: Cluster labels in blog post rows used uppercase + tracked text — replaced with a coloured dot + normal-weight 11px label
rel_2_change_7: AI Discovery Agent card now persists state across page refreshes using localStorage (key: dac_audit_v2) — restores instantly on mount then fetches fresh data in the background
rel_2_change_8: Discovery Agent “Generate manifest” state was lost on page refresh — generated manifests are now written to localStorage on success so the Listed status survives navigation
rel_2_change_9: AI Discovery audit scoring upgraded to 10-point model: +4 for URL endpoint returning 200, +2 for structured data configured and valid, +4 for probe question returning AskBiz HIT. Previous model capped at 7/10 regardless of probe results
rel_2_change_10: Discovery audit now runs probe questions across all 6 platforms in parallel (was limited to first 4), and probe hit results feed directly into platform scoring
rel_3_summary: Smart notification system, Paystack payment tracking, POS inventory archiving, CFO dashboard cleanup, and blog + academy design polish.
rel_3_change_0: In-app notification inbox — payment failures, reorder alerts, daily sales summaries, large sale flags, and source connection health all fire as notifications
rel_3_change_1: Paystack webhook on main app — charge.success and charge.failed events update payment status and create instant notifications
rel_3_change_2: Received Payments section — confirmed digital payments (M-Pesa, card) now shown separately from failed payment recovery
rel_3_change_3: Payment Recovery dashboard — dedicated API for failed/cancelled payments with recovery stats and retry tracking
rel_3_change_4: Archive button on out-of-stock items — hide products from stock alerts and Pulse without deleting them
rel_3_change_5: Shared notification helper with built-in deduplication — prevents the same alert from firing more than once per configurable window
rel_3_change_6: Source connection health alerts — stale sync warnings, disconnection alerts, and new connection confirmations
rel_3_change_7: POS daily sales summary notifications — yesterday’s revenue, transaction count, and cash vs digital breakdown delivered each morning
rel_3_change_8: Pulse dismissed signals now persist across page refreshes — signal IDs are stable instead of regenerating on every load
rel_3_change_9: Stock replenishment cron now creates notifications for critical and high-urgency reorder suggestions
rel_3_change_10: Proactive cron flags large individual sales when top sale exceeds 2× the daily average
rel_3_change_11: Paystack payments not appearing in Payment Recovery — webhook was only on pos.askbiz.co, now also on askbiz.co
rel_3_change_12: Pending payments incorrectly shown as “Retrying” — dunning API now only queries genuinely failed payments
rel_3_change_13: Dismissed Pulse alerts reappearing on refresh — removed Date.now() suffix from signal IDs
rel_3_change_14: Inline alert banners on CFO Dashboard — margin and stock alerts now handled by the notification system and Pulse
rel_3_change_15: Blog index — Popular Topics redesigned with 3 featured cards plus compact inline buttons, breaking the identical card grid
rel_3_change_16: Blog article — removed 6 interstitials (Key Insight, newsletter CTA, cluster CTA, duplicate Key Takeaways, lead magnet) leaving one clean bottom CTA
rel_3_change_17: Blog article — removed side-stripe accent borders from intro and contextual links sections
rel_3_change_18: Blog post rows — stripped badge overload (content type, difficulty, pillar pills) down to plain cluster label
rel_3_change_19: Academy index — category grid redesigned with 5 featured cards plus compact inline buttons for remaining 20 categories
rel_3_change_20: Academy article — removed mid-article gradient CTA interstitial, sidebar CTA is now the single conversion ask
rel_3_change_21: Academy article — removed side-stripe border from description and fixed uppercase tracked headings
rel_3_change_22: Academy article rows — stripped badge overload (category + difficulty pills) down to plain category label
rel_3_change_23: Font fallback inconsistency across blog and academy — all changed from sans-serif to system-ui
rel_4_summary: Critical Shopify OAuth fix, proper 404 status codes for dynamic pages, and 3-month free trial promotion on landing page.
rel_4_change_0: Shopify OAuth route returned 500 due to variable used before declaration — prevented store connection during app review
rel_4_change_1: Blog posts with invalid slugs returned HTTP 200 instead of 404 — now correctly returns 404 via Next.js notFound()
rel_4_change_2: Point of Sale sector pages returned HTTP 200 for nonexistent sectors — now returns proper 404
rel_4_change_3: Point of Sale feature pages returned HTTP 200 for nonexistent features — now returns proper 404
rel_4_change_4: Landing page hero — added prominent purple “3 months free — no card required” banner above the fold
rel_4_change_5: Landing page CTA updated from “Start free — no card needed” to “Start 3-month free trial”
rel_4_change_6: Pricing section Growth card badge changed from “Most popular” to “3 months free trial” with purple gradient
rel_4_change_7: Growth plan CTA in pricing section updated to “Start free trial” with direct signup link
rel_4_change_8: Trust badges, subtitle, and geo sub-text all updated to reflect the 3-month free trial offer
rel_4_change_9: FAQ updated to mention 3-month free trial for Growth and PoS
rel_5_summary: 3-month free trials for Growth plan and Point of Sale — no card required. Works for both Stripe and M-Pesa/PesaPal users.
rel_5_change_0: 3-month free trial for Growth plan — full Growth features (unlimited questions, daily brief, social commerce, churn intelligence) with no card upfront
rel_5_change_1: 3-month free trial for Point of Sale — up to 5 seats included, no card or M-Pesa required
rel_5_change_2: Trials table in database — tracks trial type, start/end dates, and conversion status per user
rel_5_change_3: Trial activation API — POST /api/billing with action “start_trial” for both PoS and Growth trials
rel_5_change_4: Billing page trial UI — start buttons for eligible users, active trial badges with days remaining, expired trial notices
rel_5_change_5: Trial conversion tracking — Stripe webhook and PesaPal callback both mark trials as converted on payment
rel_5_change_6: Billing API GET now fetches trial state, auto-expires trials, and returns trial info to frontend
rel_5_change_7: PoS trial locks seat stepper to 5 seats with “included in trial” label — paid users can have up to 50
rel_5_change_8: Growth trial expiry check now correctly handles PesaPal-paid users (was only checking for Stripe subscription)
rel_5_change_9: usePlan hook extended with TrialInfo interface — components can check trial.active, trial.daysLeft, trial.expired
rel_5_change_10: Growth trial expiry would incorrectly downgrade PesaPal/M-Pesa subscribers who had no Stripe subscription ID
rel_6_summary: Point of Sale marketing page, global currency support, interactive demos, geo-aware PoS pricing, and full logistics API.
rel_6_change_0: /point-of-sale marketing page — hero, camera-first scan demo, 12-feature grid, comparison table, and per-seat pricing
rel_6_change_1: Camera-first PoS demo — animated phone mock showing scan → recognise → basket → pay in 4 steps with a progress tracker
rel_6_change_2: Staff setup walkthrough — interactive screen-recording style demo showing how to add a cashier in under 2 minutes with OTP login
rel_6_change_3: Global currency support on /point-of-sale — 150+ currencies, localisation, mobile money (M-Pesa, MTN, Airtel), and 10 tax regimes (UK VAT, US Sales Tax, FIRS, KRA, GST, SARS, UAE VAT, AU GST, EU OSS, Custom)
rel_6_change_5: /benchmarks and /case-studies pages now live — were returning 404 (untracked files fixed)
rel_6_change_6: Pricing section — PoS add-on card now shown first before intelligence tiers; includes feature pills and dual CTAs
rel_6_change_7: Geo-aware PoS pricing — PoS seat price now localised per country (KSh 600, ₦2,500, R 90, ₹400, £5, $5, etc.) across all pricing surfaces and FAQ
rel_6_change_8: Growth and Business plan prices now correctly use geo-detected currency on landing page (was hardcoded £ regardless of country)
rel_6_change_9: /free-tools/break-even-calculator and /free-tools/vat-calculator were returning 404 — files committed and deployed
rel_6_change_10: PoS pricing card showing £5 for Kenyan users instead of KSh 600 — currency detection now wired end-to-end
rel_7_summary: New free tools, case studies, industry benchmarks, geo-aware CTAs, and POS seat pricing on the landing page.
rel_7_change_0: VAT Calculator — supports 30+ countries with auto-filled VAT rates and reverse VAT calculation
rel_7_change_1: Break-Even Calculator — 12 currencies, interactive chart, and what-if price scenarios
rel_7_change_2: Case Studies section — 6 real-world stories showing how SMEs use AskBiz across sectors
rel_7_change_3: SME Industry Benchmarks — compare margins, growth, AOV, and KPIs across 8 sectors (updated quarterly)
rel_7_change_4: Geo-aware CTAs — landing page adapts headline and promo for visitors from Singapore, Australia, Nigeria, and more
rel_7_change_5: African market promo banner — auto-surfaces ASKBIZ-AFRICA discount code for eligible countries
rel_7_change_6: Pricing section now shows explicit POS seat pricing (£5/seat/month add-on) on Growth and Business tiers
rel_7_change_7: Navigation mega-menu updated with Resources dropdown linking to all new sections
rel_7_change_8: Footer updated with Case Studies and Benchmarks links
rel_7_change_9: Sitemap expanded with all new routes for improved SEO coverage
rel_8_summary: Introducing AskBiz POS — a fully integrated point of sale system with camera scanning, inventory management, staff roles, and real-time BI.
rel_8_change_0: AskBiz POS — built-in point of sale system accessible from any smartphone or tablet
rel_8_change_1: Camera-based barcode and price tag scanning using your phone camera — no extra hardware needed
rel_8_change_2: Full checkout flow: scan, cart, payment (cash or card), and receipt in under 60 seconds
rel_8_change_3: WhatsApp receipt delivery — customers get digital proof of purchase instantly
rel_8_change_4: Staff management with role-based access (cashier and inventory roles) and magic link login
rel_8_change_5: Real-time inventory management with low-stock and out-of-stock alerts
rel_8_change_6: Full and partial refund handling with reason tracking and audit trail
rel_8_change_7: Transaction amendments and corrections with complete audit logging
rel_8_change_8: VAT calculation on every transaction with MTD-compatible digital record-keeping
rel_8_change_10: POS data feeds directly into dashboards, Daily Brief, Business Pulse, and AI chat
rel_8_change_11: Multi-location POS support with per-location staff, inventory, and reporting
rel_8_change_12: Offline mode — continue processing cash sales during brief internet outages
rel_8_change_13: 25 new Academy articles on POS and retail intelligence
rel_8_change_14: 22 new Help Centre articles including compliance guides (HMRC, GDPR, receipt law)
rel_8_change_15: “Retail POS Mastery” learning path — from first sale to full intelligence
rel_8_change_16: Help Centre POS integration reference updated (previously CSV-only for Zettle/Square)
rel_9_summary: Major help centre overhaul with contextual search, smart escalation, and an in-app help widget.
rel_9_change_0: In-app floating help widget — contextual article suggestions based on current page
rel_9_change_1: Live search autocomplete with article title and topic suggestions
rel_9_change_2: Sidebar topic filter on the Help Centre
rel_9_change_3: CMD+K shortcut to focus the help search from anywhere
rel_9_change_4: “New” badge on articles updated within the last 45 days
rel_9_change_5: Smart escalation after low-rating feedback — email, call booking, FAQ
rel_9_change_6: “Not helpful” reason categories: Inaccurate, Outdated, Too technical, Missing info
rel_9_change_7: Article “last verified” badge showing review month and year
rel_9_change_8: Community section on help home: forum, support call, email, FAQ
rel_9_change_9: Status banner for incident announcements across all help pages
rel_9_change_10: Article reading progress bar and back-to-top button
rel_9_change_11: Click-to-copy on inline code snippets in articles
rel_9_change_12: Video embed support for articles with a video guide
rel_9_change_13: Print/PDF view hides navigation and sidebar for clean output
rel_10_summary: Dashboard performance improvements and Shopify sync reliability fixes.
rel_10_change_0: Dashboard load time reduced by ~40% for accounts with 3+ connected sources
rel_10_change_1: Shopify order sync now retries automatically on rate-limit errors
rel_10_change_2: Fixed a bug where revenue figures could show as $0 for the current day
rel_10_change_3: Resolved an issue with date filters not persisting across page refreshes
rel_11_summary: Forecasting accuracy improvements and minor UI polish.
rel_11_change_0: Forecasting model now incorporates seasonal trends for more accurate projections
rel_11_change_1: Ask AI now remembers context across follow-up questions in the same session
rel_11_change_2: Fixed export button not working on Firefox 124
rel_11_change_3: Resolved sidebar overlap on screens narrower than 375px
rel_12_summary: Launched Business Tools v2 with new integrations and a redesigned tools sidebar.
rel_12_change_0: Business Tools v2 — redesigned sidebar with search and favourites
rel_12_change_1: Xero integration now supports multi-currency accounts
rel_12_change_2: New “Profit Pulse” widget on the home dashboard
rel_12_change_3: Google Analytics integration now pulls session and conversion data
rel_12_change_4: Removed legacy CSV importer — replaced by the new Data Import wizard
rel_13_change_0: Upgraded authentication library to address two low-severity CVEs
rel_13_change_1: Fixed chart tooltips disappearing on touch devices
rel_13_change_2: Resolved an issue where invited users could land on a blank screen after sign-up
rel_14_summary: TikTok Shop integration, CFO intelligence fixes, POS map markers, and scout agent reliability.
rel_14_change_0: TikTok Shop — connect your TikTok Shop store via QR code OAuth 2.0 flow; scan the code on your phone, approve access, and your orders sync automatically
rel_14_change_1: CFO Forecasts — projected revenue and net profit now show numbers when you have 1 month of history instead of showing dashes; horizon buttons (1M / 3M / 6M / 12M) now visibly change the projected totals
rel_14_change_2: CFO dashboard — KPI card renamed from 'Net Cash Flow' to 'Net Profit' to correctly reflect what the figure represents
rel_14_change_3: P&L comparison — net profit comparison period now includes tracked expenses so the 'vs last period' figure is a fair apples-to-apples comparison
rel_14_change_4: Market climate signal cards — fixed cards showing years (2025, 2026) or product dimensions (500 from '500ml') as the headline value; price cards now extract currency-anchored figures, and route cards show Normal / Elevated / Disrupted instead of a meaningless number
rel_14_change_5: Market climate sourcing questionnaire — after saving a sourcing answer the widget now advances to the next question instead of staying frozen on the saved confirmation
rel_14_change_6: POS map — GPS location markers now render reliably on first load; fixed a race condition where the Leaflet library finished initialising after the marker draw call
rel_14_change_7: Expenses tab — date period filter (Today, This Week, This Month etc.) now correctly filters receipts and expenses to match the selected period
rel_14_change_8: Expenses tab — removed duplicate camera emoji that was appearing on the Scan Receipt button on mobile
rel_14_change_9: Scout agents — Maya and Carolyne now search across 5 topics per run instead of 1, dramatically improving the chance of finding relevant market intelligence
rel_14_change_10: Scout agents — fixed 'Unknown error' showing in the agent log when a scout skipped because it had already run today; the log now shows a clear skip reason
rel_14_change_11: POS preview tool — Landed Cost now shows correctly for POS items even when the cost price field is null
rel_16_change_21: Welcome and re-engagement emails, with one-click unsubscribe on every send.
